# doma

> Directory tag manager — put your directories on the cutting board (Crystal CLI)

This is documentation for doma, an open-source directory tag manager written in Crystal. Content is provided under the MIT license.

## Pages

- [doma](https://doma.hahwul.com/): Directory tag manager — put your directories on the cutting board

## AI agents

- [AI skill](https://doma.hahwul.com/ai/skill/): The bundled SKILL.md teaches a coding agent when to query doma and when to skip it.

## Reference

- [Commands](https://doma.hahwul.com/reference/commands/): All doma subcommands at a glance.
- [Configuration](https://doma.hahwul.com/reference/config/): Schema for ~/.config/doma/config.yml.
- [Environment](https://doma.hahwul.com/reference/env/): Environment variables doma honors.
- [Troubleshooting](https://doma.hahwul.com/reference/troubleshooting/): Common errors, diagnostic flow, and recovery paths.

## Start

- [Comparison](https://doma.hahwul.com/start/comparison/): How doma differs from zoxide, autojump, z, fasd, and fzf.
- [Concepts](https://doma.hahwul.com/start/concepts/): How doma thinks: paths as the unit, tags as labels, frecency, soft-delete.
- [Installation](https://doma.hahwul.com/start/installation/): Install doma via Homebrew, AUR, Snap, prebuilt binary, container, or from source.
- [Quickstart](https://doma.hahwul.com/start/quickstart/): Tag a directory, inspect it, navigate to it, and operate on the whole pile.
- [Shell integration](https://doma.hahwul.com/start/shell-integration/): Make doma cd actually change your shell's working directory.

## Usage

- [Navigation](https://doma.hahwul.com/usage/navigation/): Resolve a tagged directory to a single path with list --pick, then cd to it via the shell wrapper.
- [Pipelines](https://doma.hahwul.com/usage/pipelines/): Compose doma with xargs, jq, and the rest of your shell toolbox.
- [Recipes](https://doma.hahwul.com/usage/recipes/): Real-world workflows that combine doma with the rest of your toolkit.
- [Bulk run](https://doma.hahwul.com/usage/run/): Execute a command in every directory under a tag, sequentially or in parallel.
- [Search & stats](https://doma.hahwul.com/usage/search-and-stats/): Substring search across paths and tags, plus the stats summary.
- [Snapshots](https://doma.hahwul.com/usage/snapshots/): Export and import doma's database — for backup, version control, or moving to a new machine.
- [Git status](https://doma.hahwul.com/usage/status/): See the working-tree state of every repo under a tag at a glance.
- [Tagging directories](https://doma.hahwul.com/usage/tagging/): Add tags to directories, derive them automatically from basename or git remote, and manage them as your collection grows.
- [TTL & marks](https://doma.hahwul.com/usage/ttl-and-marks/): Temporary tags that expire on their own, and the mark shorthand for the everyday case.
